title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
summary: A 4B model finetuned with RL on tool discipline (first calling get_table_name, inspecting schema, and self-correcting on errors) outperformed a 235B model on database query tasks. The approach transfers from single-table training to multi-table problems and costs under $500, demonstrating that behavioral discipline matters more than scale for this class of structured-query tasks.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel walks through finetuning a 4B model with reinforcement learning to enforce tool discipline on database queries, training it to call get_table_name, inspect schema, and self-correct after errors. The case matters because the small model outperformed Qwen 3 235B, which hallucinated on the same task, at a training cost under $500. Key takeaways: behavior shaping through RL can beat raw scale on structured tool use, and single-table training transferred to multi-table problems, raising FinQA results from 13.9% to 26.6%.
